User provisioning through SCIM
Automatically invite members to join your organization when you grant access on your IdP. If you remove a member's access to your GitHub organization on your SAML IdP, the member will be automatically removed from the GitHub organization.

“
GitHub is the world’s mono repository, so sharing our open source there is natural.”
— Martin Andersen, VP of Engineering, Trustpilot
Read the case study
“
GitHub Advanced Security is there for every pull request and excels compared to other static analysis tools we have used.”
— Dimosthenis Kaponis, CTO, Netdata
Read the case study
“
GitHub keeps us up to speed with the industry’s best tools. We want new hires to know GitHub is in our toolchain—it makes them excited to join us.”
— Spencer Kaiser, Principal Architect of Emerging Tech, American Airlines
Read the case study
“
This collaborative way of building software is unstoppable. It isn’t going away—and GitHub has its place in that. We can make the whole company rethink how they build software.”
— Ingo Sauerzapf, SAP Cloud Development Tools Manager
Read the case study
“
People know what a pull request is because it’s how they contribute to open source projects. We have many developers who are well-versed with GitHub, either for personal development or previous roles. With GitHub Enterprise, no one has to relearn the wheel.”
— Laurent Ploix, Product Manager, Spotify

GitHub Issues
/
Projects
Planning and tracking with Projects
Build adaptable projects to track your work on GitHub.
A project is an adaptable collection of items that you can view as a table, a kanban board, or a roadmap and that stays up-to-date with GitHub data. Your projects can track issues, pull requests, and ideas that you note down.
You can create and customize multiple views by filtering, sorting, and grouping issues and pull requests, visualize work with configurable charts, and add custom fields to track metadata specific to your team. Rather than enforcing a specific methodology, a project provides flexible features you can customize to your team’s needs and processes.

analysis.name: GitHub Projects
analysis.tagline: Build adaptable projects to track your work on GitHub
analysis.value_proposition: Native GitHub project management that keeps issues, PRs, and planning in one place with flexible table, board, and roadmap views that stay automatically in sync with your code.
analysis.ideal_customer: Software development teams already on GitHub who want embedded project management without a separate tool or context switch

features.name: table view
features.description: Spreadsheet-style layout showing all project items with sortable, customizable columns
features.evidence_quote: A project is an adaptable collection of items that you can view as a table, a kanban board, or a roadmap

features.name: kanban board view
features.description: Column-based board layout for visualizing work by status or custom field value
features.evidence_quote: A project is an adaptable collection of items that you can view as a table, a kanban board, or a roadmap

features.name: roadmap view
features.description: Timeline/Gantt-style view for visualizing work across date ranges
features.evidence_quote: A project is an adaptable collection of items that you can view as a table, a kanban board, or a roadmap

features.name: multiple views
features.description: Create multiple saved views per project each with independent layout, filters, and sorts
features.evidence_quote: You can create and customize multiple views by filtering, sorting, and grouping issues and pull requests

features.name: custom fields
features.description: Define team-specific metadata fields on project items beyond default issue fields
features.evidence_quote: add custom fields to track metadata specific to your team

features.name: text and number fields
features.description: Free-text and numeric custom field types for project items

features.name: date fields
features.description: Date picker custom field type for deadlines and scheduling

features.name: single select fields
features.description: Dropdown enum custom field for categorical labels or status values

features.name: iteration fields
features.description: Sprint/cycle custom field type supporting time-boxed planning periods

features.name: issue type field
features.description: Field for categorizing issues by type (e.g. bug, feature, task)

features.name: timestamp fields
features.description: Timestamp custom fields for recording time events on project items

features.name: issue fields in projects
features.description: Native issue metadata (state, assignee, labels) surfaced directly in project views

features.name: pull request fields
features.description: PR-specific metadata (review status, merge state, CI status) surfaced in project views

features.name: sub-issues
features.description: Hierarchical child issues nested under parent issues for breaking down work

features.name: sub-issue progress fields
features.description: Auto-calculated completion percentage fields derived from child issue states
features.evidence_quote: About parent issue and sub-issue progress fields

features.name: issue dependencies
features.description: Define blocking and blocked-by relationships between issues

features.name: filtering
features.description: Filter project views by any field value, assignee, label, milestone, or status

features.name: sorting
features.description: Sort items by any column or field in ascending or descending order
features.evidence_quote: You can create and customize multiple views by filtering, sorting, and grouping issues

features.name: grouping
features.description: Group items by any field value to create swimlanes or collapsible sections
features.evidence_quote: You can create and customize multiple views by filtering, sorting, and grouping issues

features.name: insights and charts
features.description: Configurable charts built from project data for burn-up, status distribution, and progress visualization

features.name: built-in automations
features.description: No-code automation rules that update item fields on trigger events (e.g. closed issue → done status)

features.name: api-based automation
features.description: GraphQL API for programmatic management of project items, fields, and views

features.name: github actions automation
features.description: Automate project item updates via GitHub Actions workflow steps

features.name: auto-add items
features.description: Automatically add issues or PRs matching criteria to a project when they are created

features.name: auto-archive items
features.description: Automatically archive items when they meet defined conditions such as being closed

features.name: draft issues
features.description: Lightweight in-project placeholder items created before being promoted to full tracked issues

features.name: labels
features.description: Color-coded text tags for categorizing and filtering issues and PRs

features.name: milestones
features.description: Group issues and PRs into versioned milestones with due dates and completion progress

features.name: project templates
features.description: Create and manage reusable project structures as templates at the organization level
features.evidence_quote: Managing project templates in your organization

features.name: copy project
features.description: Duplicate an existing project including its views, fields, and settings as a starting point

features.name: project visibility management
features.description: Toggle project between public and private visibility

features.name: project access management
features.description: Control collaborator roles (read/write/admin) on a per-project basis

features.name: export project data
features.description: Export project contents (CSV format) for offline analysis or external reporting

features.name: project linked to repository
features.description: Associate a project with a repository so it surfaces on the repo's Projects tab
features.evidence_quote: Adding your project to a repository

features.name: project linked to team
features.description: Associate a project with a GitHub team for team-wide visibility and access
features.evidence_quote: Adding your project to a team

features.name: sharing project updates
features.description: Compose and post status updates to communicate project progress to stakeholders

features.name: issue assignment
features.description: Assign issues and pull requests to one or more team members

features.name: issue pinning
features.description: Pin important issues to the top of the issue list for high-visibility tracking

features.name: issue branch creation
features.description: Create a development branch directly from an issue to start work immediately

features.name: pr-to-issue linking
features.description: Link pull requests to issues so they auto-close the referenced issue on merge

features.name: duplicate detection
features.description: Mark issues or PRs as duplicates of another existing item
features.evidence_quote: Marking issues or pull requests as a duplicate

features.name: issue transfer
features.description: Move an issue from one repository to another while preserving its history

features.name: slash commands
features.description: In-issue editor slash command shortcuts for quickly inserting content or triggering actions

features.name: issue type management
features.description: Create and manage custom issue type taxonomies at the organization level

features.name: issue field management
features.description: Define and manage structured fields on issues org-wide beyond project-level fields

features.name: issue triage
features.description: Mark and manage triage state on incoming issues for prioritization workflows

features.name: issue clone
features.description: Clone an existing issue as a starting point for a new one

features.name: semantic issue search via copilot
features.description: Natural language semantic search over issues powered by GitHub Copilot Chat
features.evidence_quote: Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat

features.name: issues & projects in free tier
features.description: Full project management features available to all users at no cost
features.evidence_quote: Issues & Projects: Give your developers flexible features for project management that adapts to any team

differentiators: Project management lives natively alongside code — no context switching between tools
Rather than enforcing a specific methodology, provides flexible features customizable to any team's needs
Projects stay automatically up-to-date with GitHub data (issues, PRs, CI status)
Included in the free tier — no additional cost for core planning features
Adapts to any team, project, and workflow
Single platform from code to deployment to planning

recent_ships.what: Issue fields in public preview for all organizations
recent_ships.source: Issue fields are now in public preview for all organizations

recent_ships.what: Timestamp fields added to GitHub Projects
Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat (projects & issues tagged)
recent_ships.source: Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat

praised: Customizable, flexible tool for planning and tracking work (HN thread, 165 upvotes)

free_tier.limits: unlimited repos; 2,000 CI/CD min/month; 500MB packages storage; community support only

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited public/private repos, Issues & Projects, Dependabot, 2k CI/CD min, 500MB packages, community support

tiers.name: Team
tiers.headline_inclusions: Everything in Free + Codespaces, repo rules, draft PRs, code owners, required reviewers, multiple PR reviewers

pricing.notes: $4/seat/month is a promotional rate for first 12 months (asterisk noted on pricing page). Pricing update banner present: 'Pricing update coming soon.' Enterprise bundles Advanced Security and Copilot as paid add-ons. GitHub Actions CI/CD minutes and Packages storage are usage-based beyond free tier limits.
